statement.markdown:
Given an incomplete Sudoku board, write a function that fills in missing numbers such that the resulting board forms a valid Sudoku solution. The solution can be any valid Sudoku configuration. Note that the input board contains either '.' representing empty cells or a digit (1-9) representing a filled cell.

Example:
 . 6 8
 8 3 .
 4 . 5
 
Hint: This is a classic use of the backtracking algorithm.
statement.inputFormat: A 9x9 2D array representation of an incomplete Sudoku board
statement.outputFormat: A 9x9 2D array representation of a valid Sudoku solution
statement.notes: The input board will always be a 9x9 grid.
